violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken cutlets were placed hot in the refrigerator and covered. Cooling methods reviewed with person in charge. Chicken was uncovered to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Nuts packaged in the food establishment are lacking the proper labels (missing business name and address). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients with major allergens, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 01/20/2026

Raw animal foods (such as pork) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as milk) in the retail dairy cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm) in the spray bottles. A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Turkey was placed hot and wrapped in the refrigerator. PIC removed plastic wrap from turkey to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024

Various items packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Labeling handouts left with person in charge at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/04/2024
